mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the labor market signals impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off positioning and equity sell-offs. When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a rolling three-month window, the S&P 500 experiences negative forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in fewer than 40% of triggered instances over a 5-year out-of-sample test, or the median 20d return in triggered windows is positive.

mechanism_narrative: Strong consumer credit expansion combined with robust housing starts indicates resilient aggregate demand, supporting corporate revenue growth and equity valuations. When RSAFS and HOUST both exceed their respective 12-month moving averages simultaneously, the S&P 500 generates positive forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative median 60-day forward return in more than 50% of occurrences, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows a statistically insignificant coefficient (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of tight labor markets, supply-side constraints drive input cost inflation, but equity markets initially price in pricing power and margin resilience. When INDPRO rises while UNRATE remains below 4.5%, the S&P 500 exhibits positive forward returns over the next 5 trading days due to short-term momentum and earnings revision upgrades.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails to predict positive 5-day returns when INDPRO growth decelerates below 0.5% month-over-month, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 52% in a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth serves as the fundamental anchor for corporate earnings trajectories. A sequential increase in GDPC1 consistently drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days as analysts upgrade earnings per share estimates in response to expanding economic activity.
failure_condition: The rolling 60-day forward return median is negative or statistically indistinguishable from zero across three consecutive non-overlapping 5-year periods.

mechanism_narrative: Aggressive monetary tightening compresses liquidity and elevates the risk premium demanded by equity investors. When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, the S&P 500 experiences negative forward returns over the next 5 trading days as valuation multiples contract and growth-sensitive sectors underperform.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or the cross-sectional correlation between rate levels and index returns becomes positive over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between payroll growth and unemployment rates signals a cooling labor market that precedes broader economic slowdown. When PAYEMS growth decelerates while UNRATE rises simultaneously, the S&P 500 generates negative forward returns over the next 20 trading days as recession risks price into equity valuations.
failure_condition: The joint deterioration signal yields positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of observed instances, or the interaction coefficient in a panel regression fails to reach statistical significance (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In post-shock recovery environments, fiscal and monetary stimulus initially boosts consumer credit and housing activity, driving equity outperformance. When RSAFS expands rapidly alongside rising HOUST, the S&P 500 produces positive forward returns over the next 60 trading days as demand-side recovery fuels earnings revisions.
failure_condition: The forward return median turns negative when RSAFS growth decelerates below 2% quarter-over-quarter, or the signal's information ratio falls below 0.1 over a 3-year out-of-sample evaluation.
